name: Chicken with Ginger Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 Whole chicken breasts, skinned, boned, halved
3 oz Fresh oyster mushrooms, thinly sliced
1/2 large Red bell pepper, cut julienne
1 tsp Potato starch dissolved in 1 Tb cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Blend lime juice and several drops of hot pepper sauce in a large bowl.
2. Add chicken to the mixture and marinate for at least 4 hours at room temperature, turning frequently.
3. With the blender running, add peanut oil, ginger, honey, and peppercorns; mix until smooth.
4. Add vinegar, water, soy sauce, basil, and garlic; blend until smooth.
5. With the machine still running, add Oriental sesame oil and blend until the sauce is smooth.
6. Drain chicken and pat dry; cut into 1/2-inch pieces.
7. Heat unrefined sesame oil in a large heavy skillet over medium-high heat.
8. Add chicken and stir-fry for 1 minute.
9. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until the chicken is almost tender, about 1 to 2 minutes.
10. Add sauce, mushrooms, green onions, bell pepper, and potato starch; stir until thickened and heated through, about 2 minutes.
11. Spoon onto warmed plates and garnish with kale leaves and lime halves.
12. Serve with steamed broccoli.
